title: Purged Race-Walk Validation With Next-Lap Embargo
summary: Because the label is 'pit on next lap', adjacent laps from the same driver/stint are near-duplicates; ordinary grouped CV can overstate AUC if training rows sit immediately before or after test events.
rationale: Because the label is 'pit on next lap', adjacent laps from the same driver/stint are near-duplicates; ordinary grouped CV can overstate AUC if training rows sit immediately before or after test events. Walk-forward splits with a small temporal embargo are standard leakage protection for forward-horizon labels.
implementation_hint: Split by Year/Race in chronological order; inside each held-out race, purge training rows from the same driver within +/-1 lap of test laps and optionally embargo the first post-test lap. Compare current CV to purged CV before selecting features/models.
expected_effect: Lower but more trustworthy local CV; better correlation between local ranking and public LB, especially for aggressive contextual features.
risk: If the test set is IID rather than future-like, stricter CV may underuse data and slightly slow iteration.
